title: Barrio to Burbs: The Making of the Mexican American Middle Class By Jody Agius Vallejo Stanford University Press. 2012. 248 pages. $24.95 paperback, $40.00 cloth
abstract: Studies on the Latino middle class are a burgeoning part of the Latino/a sociology subfield. Jodi Agius Vallejo's book Barrio to Burbs: The Making of the Mexican American Middle Class provides significant insight into the experiences of Latinos of Mexican American ancestry living in the United States. Her arguments also provide significant understanding of the changing face of US immigrants and their means for incorporation into US society. Vallejo recognizes that with the significant growth of the Mexican American population comes a significant growth in the middle-class portion of this group as well. With a qualitative approach encompassing 80 in-depth interviews and an extensive ethnography, the author provides a lens for thinking about how these Mexican Americans' class status significantly impacts their access to the resources once thought to be accessible only by Anglos. Drawing on contemporary theories of upward mobility, her discussion covers numerous topics regarding this cross section of the Mexican American population, such as identity, race, networks, and social stratification.
